PN_Action_Taxonomy.goal: Establish early alignment and confirm referral logistics

action_items: Confirm insurance eligibility (Straight Medicare vs. Advantage)
Verify diagnosis meets home care criteria
primary_intents: Ensure qualification before introducing services

action_items: Gather DOB, phone, address, and POA details
Check distance for Medical Assistant (MA) travel
Audit face sheets for address errors (cross-check Atlantis data against facility face sheet before HHA referral)
Audit Atlantis demographics (verify insurance, address, phone #, and DOB accuracy in Atlantis queue)
primary_intents: Readiness for immediate family communication

action_items: Meet patient in-person within first 48 hours
Shadow the Traditional Provider (TP) to get facility entry
primary_intents: Gain trust and prepare patient for transition

action_items: Contact Social Worker (SW) and TP immediately upon assignment
Confirm referral receipt with HHA intake coordinator
Verify internal 'Hub' receipt (confirm the referral was received and logged in the Hub system)
primary_intents: Maintain relationship for early discharge info

action_items: Ask Q1: 'Have you heard of [our company]?'
Ask Q2: 'Do you know why we are here?'
Ask Q3: 'Has anyone explained home health services to you?'
Ask Q4: 'Do you have any concerns about going home?'
Document patient responses in stage_1_intake_responses
If Q4 surfaces specific barriers (no caregiver, unsafe home, patient anxiety), escalate to SW immediately
primary_intents: Establish baseline patient awareness and anxiety level
Identify early barriers that require SW intervention

PN_Action_Taxonomy.goal: Track progress, educate family, and escalate barriers to the appropriate professional

action_items: Track discharge timeline updates relayed by SW or facility liaison
Note wound care progress from provider updates shared by the SW
primary_intents: Ensure awareness of barriers or changes in progress without accessing clinical systems directly

action_items: Maintain weekly communication with staff/SW
Request post-conference summary from SW regarding discharge timeline changes
primary_intents: Ensure immediate updates if discharge plans shift

action_items: Educate patient/family on program benefits
Discuss POA awareness of home services
Walk family through Day 1 expectations at home: who arrives, what equipment should be present, and what to do if the HHA nurse is late
Insert V-Card into patient's phone and deliver program flyer to patient/family
primary_intents: Prevent 'missed' discharge or refusal
Reduce caregiver anxiety by setting concrete expectations

action_items: Learn PCP and HHA preferences well in advance
Identify family support/training needs
primary_intents: Proactive transition planning

action_items: Escalate clinical or insurance concerns to the Social Worker rather than attempting resolution directly
Flag volatile family situations to SW for immediate intervention
Escalate weekend coverage gaps to the SW (flag Friday discharges with no weekend HHA coverage so SW can adjust the discharge date or arrange bridging)
primary_intents: Ensure complex issues are handled by the appropriate professional

PN_Action_Taxonomy.goal: Deliver a 'Maximum Reward' handoff and secure service adoption

action_items: Verify with SW the confirmed D/C date and selected HHA before coordinating logistics
Confirm no address/contact changes
primary_intents: Ensure alignment with all transition providers

action_items: Pass contact/address info and first appointment availability to home team
Verify HHA has confirmed the start-of-care date, assigned nurse name, and expected first-visit time window
primary_intents: Prepare home team for continuity of care

action_items: Conduct 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call to patient/family to confirm home readiness and first-visit time
Hand physical appointment card with Care Coordinator info to patient
Confirm time works for patient/DM
Schedule the Medical Assistant (MA) for the first visit within 24 hours of discharge
primary_intents: Ensure patient feels informed and comfortable

action_items: Confirm HHA has set the SOC date
Confirm HHA has assigned a nurse for the first visit
Confirm HHA has provided the first-visit time window
Only after all three confirmations ('In Place'), proceed to schedule the MA
If HHA is NOT 'In Place', investigate the specific barrier and escalate to SW
primary_intents: Prevent premature MA scheduling that risks a wasted visit
Ensure the HHA-First Rule is satisfied before committing MA resources

action_items: When HHA is not 'In Place', call the HHA intake coordinator to identify the specific blocker
Categorize the barrier (staffing shortage, intake freeze, missing orders, SOC scheduling conflict)
Escalate the categorized barrier to the SW with a specific ask (e.g., 'Can you follow up on the missing orders?')
Document the barrier in hha_confirmation_status
primary_intents: Transform a vague 'HHA not ready' into an actionable, specific barrier
Give the SW a clear next step rather than a generic escalation

action_items: Call SW to confirm D/C date is firm and HHA has confirmed start-of-care
Call patient/family to confirm understanding of Day 1 plan (who is arriving, equipment, what to do if nurse is late)
Document both calls in stage_3_followup_audit
If either call reveals a gap, escalate before proceeding to pulse call
primary_intents: Close the Confidence Loop before the final 24-hour window
Prevent discharge-day surprises by verifying alignment across all parties

action_items: Coordinate with Regional Director (RD) and HHA to prevent same-day 'Double Dipping'
primary_intents: Ensure service payment and coverage safety
